event: The Rest on the Flight into Egypt
symbols: moonlight on water — divine guidance illuminating the path of the fugitive Holy Family
campfire in the distance — temporary shelter and warmth during the flight from danger
starry sky — God's providence watching over the travelers by night
donkey — the humble beast of burden carrying the holy travelers
description: A nocturnal landscape dominated by a dark sky with stars and a bright moon reflecting on a body of water at the right. In the center foreground, a small group of figures — a woman holding a child, accompanied by a man — travel along a path near dark silhouetted trees. A campfire glows in the distance at the left, near what appears to be a donkey and other figures. The scene is overwhelmingly dark with the moon providing the primary light source, creating a peaceful yet mysterious atmosphere.
theological_description: The Rest on the Flight into Egypt depicts the Holy Family's nocturnal journey after the angel's warning to flee Herod's murderous plot (Matthew 2:13-15). This small, intimate scene transforms a vast dark landscape into a meditation on divine providence. The God who placed the stars in the sky now guides a refugee family by their light. The moonlit path represents the narrow way of faith — barely visible, yet sufficient. The campfire suggests the temporary shelters along the journey, recalling Israel's own sojourn in Egypt and the pillar of fire that guided them. This painting captures a truth easily overlooked: the Son of God entered the world as a refugee, hunted by political power, dependent on the faith and obedience of His earthly parents. The darkness that surrounds the Holy Family is the darkness of a world that does not yet recognize its Savior.

scripture_refs.reason: Joseph warned in a dream to flee to Egypt with the child — the journey depicted
Out of Egypt I called my son — fulfilled in Christ's return from Egypt
The Lord is your keeper, the sun shall not strike you by day nor the moon by night — divine protection on the journey
When you pass through the waters I will be with you — God's presence in the family's flight

_meta.title: The Rest on the Flight into Egypt
